---
title: "ANX_bar_plot"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library('readxl')
Xanax <- read_excel('ANX_2026_daner_fullANX_v12_woUTAH_11022026.gz.p4.clump.areator.sorted.1mhc (1).xls')
```
```{r}
library('tidyverse')

chr_distances_1 <- list()

for(chr_num in 1:10){
  chr_data <- Xanax %>% 
    filter(CHR == chr_num) %>% 
    arrange(BP)
  # 2. Only calculate distances if there are at least 2 SNPs
  #    because you need a current SNP and a next SNP to find a distance
  if (nrow(chr_data) >= 2) {
    
    # 3. Create a table comparing each SNP to the next SNP
    distance_table <- data.frame(
      CHR = chr_num,                         # chromosome number
      SNP = chr_data$SNP[-nrow(chr_data)],   # all SNPs except the last one
      curr_SNP_pos = chr_data$BP[-nrow(chr_data)], # all positions except the last one
      next_SNP_pos = chr_data$BP[-1],            # all positions except the first one
      Distance = diff(chr_data$BP)           # distance between neighboring SNPs
    )
    
    # 4. Store this chromosome's distance table in the list
    chr_distances_1[[chr_num]] <- distance_table
  }
}

# 5. Combine all chromosome tables into one big data frame
chr_distances_1  <- do.call(rbind, chr_distances_1)

# 3. Classify each distance into your table's custom scales!
chr_distances_1  <- chr_distances_1  %>% 
  mutate(
    Classification = case_when(
      Distance < 20000                  ~ "very close",
      Distance >= 20000 & Distance < 100000   ~ "close",
      Distance >= 100000 & Distance < 1000000 ~ "far",
      Distance >= 1000000               ~ "very far"
    )
  )


# View the final table
chr_distances_1 
```


Above is distance table 1 (Chromosomes 1-10)
Below is distance table 2 (Chromosomes 11-22)


```{r}
#new empty list thats going to hold all chromosome distances, each entry is a dataframe
chr_distances_2 <- list()

for(chr_num in 11:22){
  chr_data <- Xanax %>% 
    filter(CHR == chr_num) %>% 
    arrange(BP)
  # 2. Only calculate distances if there are at least 2 SNPs
  #    because you need a current SNP and a next SNP to find a distance
  if (nrow(chr_data) >= 2) {
    
    # 3. Create a table comparing each SNP to the next SNP
    distance_table <- data.frame(
      CHR = chr_num,                         # chromosome number
      SNP = chr_data$SNP[-nrow(chr_data)],   # all SNPs except the last one
      curr_SNP_pos = chr_data$BP[-nrow(chr_data)], # all positions except the last one
      next_SNP_pos = chr_data$BP[-1],            # all positions except the first one
      BP_distance = diff(chr_data$BP)           # distance between neighboring SNPs
    )
    
    # 4. Store this chromosome's distance table in the list
    chr_distances_2[[chr_num]] <- distance_table
  }
}

# 5. Combine all chromosome tables into one big data frame
chr_distances_2 <- do.call(rbind, chr_distances_2)

# 3. Classify each distance into your table's custom scales!
chr_distances_2 <- chr_distances_2 %>% 
  mutate(
    Classification = case_when(
      BP_distance < 20000                  ~ "very close",
      BP_distance >= 20000 & BP_distance < 100000   ~ "close",
      BP_distance >= 100000 & BP_distance < 1000000 ~ "far",
      BP_distance >= 1000000               ~ "very far"
    )
  )


# View the final table
chr_distances_2 


```




CREATING A LIST/DICTIONARY FOR VERY CLOSE PAIRS BELOW
```{r}

closepairs <- list()
close_SNPs <- c()

#loops through chr_distances_1 and 2, pulls out rows (SNPS) classified as very close, and adds that SNP
#and the next (very close to each other) as well as the chromosome number to a list (dictionary)

for (i in 1:length(chr_distances_1$Classification)) {
  if (chr_distances_1$Classification[i] == 'very close') {
    closepairs <- append(closepairs, list(c(chr_distances_1$CHR[i], chr_distances_1$SNP[i], chr_distances_1$SNP[i+1])))
    close_SNPs <- append(volcano_SNP_overlaps, chr_distances_1$SNP[i])
  }
}


for (i in 1:length(chr_distances_2$Classification)) {
  if (chr_distances_2$Classification[i] == 'very close') {
    closepairs <- append(closepairs, list(c(chr_distances_2$CHR[i], chr_distances_2$SNP[i], chr_distances_2$SNP[i+1]))) 
    close_SNPs <- append(volcano_SNP_overlaps, chr_distances_2$SNP[i])
  }
}

closepairs



```
```{r}
 
sig_from_volcano <- c("rs58825580", "rs7110863",  "rs10959883", "rs11241568", "rs4976976",  "rs10476497", "rs77960")
overlaps <- c()
for (i in 1:length(close_SNPs)) {
  for (j in 1:length(sig_from_volcano)) {
    if (sig_from_volcano[j] == close_SNPs[i]) {
    overlaps <- append(overlaps, close_SNPs[i])
    }
  }
}
overlaps
```




---------------This is for analysis with the volcano plot------------------

```{r}
# 1. First, get your sorted positions as a data frame
chr1_data <- Xanax %>% 
  filter(CHR == 1) %>% 
  arrange(BP)

# 2. Pair each SNP position with its next neighbor and calculate the distance

SNP <- c(chr1_data$SNP[-nrow(chr1_data)])
Current_Pos <- c(chr1_data$BP[-nrow(chr1_data)]) # All positions except the very last
Next_Pos <- c(chr1_data$BP[-1])                # All positions except the very first
BP_distance <- c(diff(chr1_data$BP))            # The calculated physical distance

distance_table <- data.frame(SNP, Current_Pos, Next_Pos, Distance)
distance_table
```
```{r}
# 3. Classify each distance into your table's custom scales!
distance_table <- distance_table %>% 
  mutate(
    Classification = case_when(
      Distance < 20000                  ~ "very close",
      Distance >= 20000 & Distance < 100000   ~ "close",
      Distance >= 100000 & Distance < 1000000 ~ "far",
      Distance >= 1000000               ~ "very far"
    )
  )

distance_table
```



```{r}

```
```
